_README: Plain readable answer key, withheld from the agent's workspace. Tuned to the REAL GDPval file 'Population v2.xlsx' (1,516 KRI rows: No | Division | Sub-Division | Country | Legal Entity | KRIs | Q3 2024 KRI | Q2 2024 KRI). The grader recomputes risk criteria from this file and checks the agent's sample for precision and coverage, not an exact set.

sample_size_expectation.note: 90% confidence, 10% tolerable error. Defensible methods: AICPA attribute-sampling table (~24 with zero expected deviations) or the proportion formula z^2*p*(1-p)/e^2 (~68 at p=0.5). The judge accepts any documented, consistent method; the deterministic axis only checks a Sample Size Calculation tab exists and the sample lands in a plausible range.

correct_analysis: Compute the quarter-on-quarter variance for each KRI from Q2 and Q3 2024 (treat a zero prior quarter as a zero-value flag, not a ratio). Build a risk-based audit sample whose every member is justifiable: prioritise KRIs with greater than 20% QoQ variance, zero-value rows, and the high-risk jurisdictions present in the file (Cayman Islands, Pakistan, UAE), and ensure the 13 Division/Sub-Division combinations are represented. Document a sample-size calculation at 90% confidence and 10% tolerable error. The result is a defensible *sample* — a clear subset of the 1,516 rows, each traceable by its 'No' identifier and flagged — not the whole risky population and not an unjustified grab.
do_not_overreward: Flagging the entire population or all ~800 risky rows instead of a sized sample.
Selecting rows that meet no documented risk criterion.
A sample-size number with no method or workings; dropping the 'No' identifiers so selections cannot be traced.

axis_definitions.factual_accuracy: QoQ variance is computed correctly and every flagged row genuinely meets a stated risk criterion against the population.
axis_definitions.professional_judgment: A sensibly sized, risk-weighted sample with Division/Sub-Division and jurisdiction coverage — neither over- nor under-selecting.
axis_definitions.evidence_grounding: Every selected row is traceable to a population row by 'No'; the rationale ties to the file's actual columns.
axis_definitions.completeness: Sample Size Calculation, variance computation, and a flagged sample are all present.
axis_definitions.format_regulatory_compliance: A parseable .xlsx sample workbook retaining the identifier column and a sample flag.
axis_definitions.clarity: Workings and selection rationale are documented well enough to defend in review.

llm_judge_reference.task_goal: Score an internal-audit AFC KRI sample-selection workbook for correct, defensible, risk-based sampling against the real population file.
llm_judge_reference.hidden_reference: Top-band answers document a defensible sample-size method, compute QoQ variance, and produce a sized, risk-justified sample with full Division/Sub-Division and high-risk-jurisdiction coverage, traceable by 'No' — without flagging the whole population.
